source_metadata.title_raw: Robin D. Peters - I-18-065 - May 11, 2018 - Sutter Creek

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Robin D. Peters 
Sutter Creek Councilmember 
P.O. Box 1196 
Sutter Creek, CA 95685 
May 11, 2018 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-18-065 
Dear Mr. Peters: 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign and behested 
payment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"}. 1 Please note that we are only providing 
advice under the campaign and behested payments provisions of the Act and not under other 
prohibitions such as common Jaw conflict of interest or Section 1090. Because your question is 
general in nature, we are providing informal assistance.2 
Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
QUESTIONS 
As a Sutter Creek City Councilmember, who solicits donations in your capacity as chairman 
of the charitable organization, Knight Foundry Alliance ("KFA"): 
1. Are all aggregated donations to KF A of $5,000 or more per calendar year by any single 
source as a result of your fundraising behests reportable behested payments? 
2. Are the donations to KF A in response to a mailed solicitation reportable behested 
payments if your name is not on the mailed solicitations and you serve on the KF A 
Board of Directors? 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provi.ded by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 


File No. 1-18-065 
Page No. 2 
3. What are your reporting requirements if you are not affiliated with KF A and 
encouraged donations from friends or relatives that exceed the $5,000 threshold? 
4. If reporting is required, does it apply to any donation from anywhere that meets the 
reporting criteria or only to a donation received from donors within your jurisdiction? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. Yes, all aggregated donations to KF A of $5,000 or more per calendar year by any single 
source as a result of your fundraising behests are reportable behested payments. 
2. If the KF A mailed solicitation does not include your name or your photo in any form, 
donations in response to the solicitation are not reportable behested payments. 
3. Where the payments to K.FA are at your direction, request or suggestion, they are 
reportable behested payments if they meet the reporting criteria regardless of your 
affiliation with K.F A. 
4. Reporting is required of any donation that meets the reporting criteria. There is no 
limitatiqn as to the origin of the payment for reporting purposes. 
FACTS 
You are a City Councilmember for the City of Sutter Creek. You are also the chairman of an 
unincorporated organization, the Knight Foundry Alliance ("KF A"), operating under the umbrella 
of a fiscal sponsor, the Amador Community Foundation ("ACF"), a 50l(c)(3) non-profit 
organization. KFA's mission is to raise money and carry out the acquisition, restoration and 
continued operation of the Knight Foundry for educational and historic purposes. Through K.F A's 
efforts, sufficient revenues were raised in 2017 to complete site acquisition of a City-owned 
property. Continued fundraising is now allowing for site restoration to begin. KF A manages the 
property on the City's behalf. KF A expects to incorporate as a stand-alone non-profit organization 
during 2018. 
Fundraising efforts are funded by donations and raised revenues; the City does not 
contribute public monies to fundraising efforts. Fundraising includes mass mailings to potential 
donors, email solicitations, advertising, promotions, social media, a web presence, and word of 
mouth. You write most of the fundraising materials and your name is on most or all of the 
materials, as chairman of the organization. 
Donations to date range in magnitude from less than $5 to $85,000, with larger donations 
expected in the near future. Every penny of every donation is deposited to the non-profit sponsor, 
Amador Community Foundation. Expenses are paid from raised revenues deposited with ACF. 
KFA has no bank account and holds no money. 


File No. 1-18-065 
Page No. 3 
You believe that you are "behesting donations" to KF A and request information on your 
reporting requirements. 3 
ANALYSIS 
Behested Payments 
Under the Act, an elected official who fundraises or otherwise solicits payments from one 
individual or organization to be given to another individual or organization may be required to 
report the payment. The purpose of the "behested payment" reporting requirements is to capture 
payments that are not direct contributions to elected officials or gifts, but payments in which the 
public would have an interest given the official's role in the exchange. 
A donation to a charitable organization is "made at the behest of' an elected official if the 
payment is made "under the control or at the direction of, in cooperation, consultation, 
coordination, or concert with, at the request or suggestion of, or with the express, prior consent of' 
the elected official, or the official's agent. (Section 82041.3; Regulation 18215.3.) 
A behested payment must be reported pursuant to the requirements of Section 84224, where 
the payment(s) equal or exceed $5,000 from the same source in a calendar year within a 30-day 
timeframe. Once a single source has made a behested payment of $5,000 or more during the 
calendar year, subsequent payments of any amount from that source must be reported. 
Therefore, all aggregated donations of $5,000 or more per calendar year by any single 
source to KF A as a result of your fundraising behests are reportable. This requirement applies to all 
such payments, with no limitation as to jurisdictional origins. 
Elected Official Featured in Solicitation 
You ask whether a solicitation from KFA would result in a behested payment if you are not 
on the mailer, and you are serving on the organization's Board of Directors. The rule is that if an 
elected official, is "featured" on a solicitation, then the elected official is making a behest, and the 
payments must be reported. (Regulation 182015.3(b).) 
An elected official is "featured" on a solicitation under two circumstances. First, if the 
solicitation includes the elected officer's photograph or signature, or "singles out the elected officer by 
the manner of display of his or her name or office in the layout of the document, such as by headlines, 
captions, type size, typeface, or type color." (Regulation 18215.3(b)(l ).) Second, if the solicitation's 
roster or letterhead listing the governing body of the organization contains a majority of elected officers. 
(Regulation 18215.3(b)(2).) 
Therefore, if your name, or photo, does not appear on the KF A mailed solicitations in any 
form, the donations are not at your behest. If the solicitation inc1udes you in a list of the Board of 
Directors, and the list contains a majority of elected officers, you will be "featured" and the 
3 You have also asked whether, other city officials need to report the donations if they sit on the KF A Board of 
Directors. Generally, the bested payment rules are applicable to all elected officers. However, we can provide assistance 
only an official whose duties are in question and we do not provide hypothetical _advice. (See Regulation 18329.) 


File No. 1-18-065 
Page No. 4 
behested payments must be reported. This reporting requirement applies to all elected officials who 
"make a behest" If you encourage donations from friends and relatives, those payments would be 
"at your direction" or "at your request or suggestion" and therefore would be reportable at or above 
the $5,000 threshold for the calendar year. (Section 84224; Regulation 18215.3(a).) 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
LKH:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
Assistant General Counsel 
By: 
L. Karen Harrison 
Counsel, Legal Division
